summary: Iteration_5: full backend + frontend validation of new Recorded Sessions feature (public mobile-gated /recordings viewer + admin RecordingsManager CRUD). 11/11 new pytest cases in test_recordings.py PASS. 11/11 mobile_compat regression PASS. Frontend critical flows verified end-to-end: desktop & mobile nav 'Recordings' link, gate UX (PRIVATE CLIENT PORTAL heading + data-testid inputs), 400 short/403 unauthorized/200 authorized branches with auto-restore from localStorage 'ns_recordings_mobile', sign-out clears localStorage, admin RecordingsManager create/delete section+video flow. Backend filters videos correctly (open vs allowed_mobiles whitelist), hides empty sections, returns 403 on expired access. Existing Mobile Compatibility + Blogs + admin user CRUD are NOT regressed.

minor.issue: When expires_on is in the past, current backend code wraps the date comparison in try/except Exception:pass — so although the test mobile WAS authorized as 'active' with a past expires_on, the active query in db.authorized_users does still return it and the expiry branch correctly returns 403 with 'expired' detail in our test. This is fine, but note: any malformed date string silently falls through to 200 (mobile would be 'authorized' anyway). Consider validating expires_on at write time so the silent pass is unreachable.

issues: (Carryover) date input uses native browser picker, not shadcn calendar.

tests_executed: pytest backend/tests/test_recordings.py -> 11 passed in 5.16s (PASS): gate 400, 403 unauthorized, 200 authorized admin; admin CRUD section+video including invalid YT 400 and PATCH+GET verification; cascade delete section->videos; allowed_mobiles filtering (open visible, restricted hidden, sections with no visible videos hidden, allowed_mobiles not leaked in response); 403 expired; check-authorized regression.
Playwright: desktop nav 'Recordings' link visible and routes to /recordings (PASS).
Playwright: gate form input (recordings-mobile-input) + submit (recordings-submit-btn) + heading 'Private Client Portal' visible (PASS).
Playwright: submitted 1234567890 -> recordings-error shows backend 'not authorized' detail message (PASS).
Playwright: submitted 9929059153 -> transitioned to library view, 'Welcome back' header visible, recordings-logout-btn present (PASS).
Playwright: localStorage 'ns_recordings_mobile' = '9929059153'; reload page -> session auto-restored, logout btn still present (PASS).
Playwright: clicked recordings-logout-btn -> back to gate, localStorage cleared (PASS).
Playwright (390x844): clicked data-testid='mobile-menu-toggle' -> mobile-menu-panel opened -> Recordings link visible with href=/recordings (PASS).
Playwright admin: logged in 9929059153/2193 -> RecordingsManager (data-testid='recordings-manager') scrolled into view (PASS).
Playwright admin: rec-add-section-btn -> rec-section-form -> rec-section-name='TEST_FE_Section_iter5' -> rec-section-submit -> section row visible (PASS).

Playwright admin: rec-add-video-btn (enabled after section exists) -> rec-video-form -> rec-video-section <select> -> rec-video-title='TEST_FE_Video' -> rec-video-url='https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ' -> rec-video-submit -> video row visible with EDIT/Delete actions (PASS).

Cleanup: backend pytest cleanup fixture removed all TEST_ sections/videos/users. Leftover TEST_FE_Section_iter5 from Playwright was manually deleted via DELETE /api/admin/recordings/sections/{id} (cascade removed TEST_FE_Video).
action_items: (Test-script note, NOT an app bug) In Playwright admin flow, deleting section/video required listening to the native confirm() dialog before clicking — my first delete-section call missed the dialog due to async registration timing. The data-testids (rec-delete-section-<id>, rec-delete-video-<id>) work; manual cleanup via API confirmed cascade behaviour. If you want flaky-free CI for delete actions, consider replacing window.confirm() with an in-app modal (e.g. AlertDialog) and add data-testid='rec-delete-confirm'.
(Minor) Validate expires_on at write time (Pydantic date field) so the silent try/except Exception:pass in the expiry branch of /api/recordings/access becomes unreachable.
(Cosmetic, carryover) Replace fetchpriority -> fetchPriority on hero in /app/frontend/src/App.js.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is now 533 lines — still under the 700-line threshold but Recordings + Blogs + Admin + Compatibility share one file. When the next feature lands, split into routers (recordings_router.py, blogs_router.py, admin_router.py).
extract_youtube_id regex correctly accepts youtu.be/, youtube.com/watch?v=, /embed/, /v/, /shorts/ but NOT music.youtube.com or query params other than v=. Acceptable for current scope.
Response from /api/recordings/access correctly strips allowed_mobiles from each video before returning — verified in test_filtering_visible_and_hidden. Good privacy hygiene.
Frontend Recordings.jsx (344 lines) is a single-file component with GateForm + Library + VideoCard. Borderline; OK for now.
(Carryover) seed_admin() not idempotent on password rotation.
(Carryover) JWT stored in localStorage 'ns_admin_token' (XSS exposure) — playbook prefers httpOnly cookies.
(Carryover) FastAPI deprecated @app.on_event('startup') — migrate to lifespan handler.
seed_data_creation: All test data created during this run has been cleaned up: pytest fixture deleted TEST_QA_* sections/videos and TEST_QA_Client / TEST_Expired users; manual curl DELETE removed leftover TEST_FE_Section_iter5 (cascade removed TEST_FE_Video). Final state: only the original 'Mobile Numerology' section exists in db.video_sections.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Recordings feature is fully working end-to-end. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_recordings.py (11 cases, run with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL exported). Frontend testids on RecordingsManager: rec-add-section-btn, rec-section-form/name/submit, rec-add-video-btn, rec-video-form/section/title/url/submit, rec-edit-video-<id>, rec-delete-video-<id>, rec-delete-section-<id>. Public gate testids: recordings-mobile-input, recordings-submit-btn, recordings-error, recordings-logout-btn, recordings-section-tab-<slug>, recordings-video-<id>. localStorage key for client session: ns_recordings_mobile. NOTE for next agent: delete buttons in RecordingsManager use native window.confirm() — register the dialog handler BEFORE clicking, and prefer page.once('dialog', d => d.accept()) for the very next click.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional issues found. The 'delete failed' observation in my Playwright admin run was caused by the asyncio.create_task registration timing on the dialog event, NOT by the application. Manual API delete confirmed cascade works.
